title: Vagaari Empire
content:
{{Top|leg|canon=Vagaari pirates}} {{Otheruses|Empire}} {{Government |image= |name=Vagaari Empire |type=Nomadic empire[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] |founding= |constitution= |headofstate=[[Miskara]] |headofgov=Miskara |commander=[[Supreme Commander (Vagaari)|Supreme Commander]]''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' |executive= |legislative= |judicial= |military=[[Vagaari attack force]] |sector= |system= |celestialbody= |capital= |language=[[Vagaari (language)|Vagaari]]''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' |currency= |religious= |holiday= |anthem= |formed= |established= |fragmented= |reorganized= |dissolved= |restored= |affiliation=[[Yuuzhan Vong]] }} {{Quote|We are the Vagaari. We take what we want.|A [[Unidentified Miskara (Jorj Car'das)|Miskara]]|}} The '''Vagaari Empire''' was the [[Politics#Empire|domain]] of the [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] [[Species/Legends|species]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] who [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]] flew freely taking and destroying at will with cruelty and terror. Even other species in the Unknown Regions, such as the [[Ebruchi]] and the [[Rakatan|Rakata]] would flee its power. The Vagaari eventually adopted a nomadic lifestyle in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] at some point before [[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]], leaving their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Vagar Praxut]] almost entirely deserted. Their reign of terror was halted in [[37 BBY/Legends|37 BBY]] by the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]], and they and were forced to retreat to their own corner of the Unknown Regions. The Vagaari Empire then began building up forces for an eventual attack on the Ascendancy. The [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] became aware of the Vagaari buildup, and feared that they had "already made alliances with powers even more dangerous than they are." The Vagaari Empire began a [[Third Vagaari War|war]] against the Chiss Ascendancy in [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]]. During this war, it was revealed that Vagaari were posing as [[Geroon (species)|Geroon,]] survivors of the [[Outbound Flight]] and their [[Outbound Flight Colony|colony]] for nearly five decades. However, the [[Geroon Remnant]] would soon reveal themselves as the Vagaari, who had come to avenge their losses at the hands of the Chiss years before. The Geroon Remnant were [[Battle of the Redoubt|defeated]] in [[The Redoubt/Legends|The Redoubt]] by the Chiss Ascendancy, with the aid of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the [[New Jedi Order]] and [[Empire of the Hand]]. ==Description== ===Culture=== {{Quote|He's helping catalog the items for me. ''[…]'' Some of the systems plundered by the Vagaari are in Republic territory, and he has some knowledge of their origin and value.|Force Commander Mitth'raw'nuruodo, on Captain Dubrak Qennto||translated=1}} [[File:Vagaarimask-tur.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A Vagaari traditional mask, used to scare others.]] The Vagaari Empire was a nomadic government governed by a [[Miskara]], an individual that ruled the [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] [[Species/Legends|species]] after executing a [[Death/Legends|coup d'etat]] on the previous ruler with the support of a major cadre. Once the coup was finished, the cadre became the Empire's new aristocracy. [[Gender/Legends|Females]] formulate strategies, acquire [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]], and take care of sanitation and maintenance, while males were the [[Politics/Legends|politicians]], [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]], [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]]. The Vagaari's philosophy and religion consisted of believing [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] were cowards who were mere assets, and that their [[Deity/Legends|gods]] created them so they could have superior beings to worship. The Empire thrived on destruction, plundering worlds and species to maintain its nomadic people. As the Vagaari were not [[Diet/Legends|predators]], they dressed with voluminous [[Clothing/Legends|robes]] and tall wraparound [[Vagaari traditional mask|traditional masks]] with frightening patterns to scare their preys. [[Vagaari soldier]]s used similar clothing, wearing [[Helmet/Legends|helmets]] resembling [[Demon/Legends|demons]] and [[Armor/Legends|armor]] while in combat. ===Equipment=== {{Quote|Interesting. ''[…]'' I seem to have miscalculated.''»
«''No kidding. ''[…]'' Let's get out of here before someone sees us.''»
«''I knew the battle would be of this scale. What I hadn't realized was the Vagaari's true nature. ''[…]'' You see those other vessels? They're not here to fight. ''[…]'' Those are the civilians. ''[…]'' By the way they're grouped in defensive posture, with true war vessels set in screening positions around them. ''[…]'' The error I spoke of was that the Vagaari aren' t simply a strong, well-organized pirate force. They're a completely nomadic species.|Force Commander Mitth'raw'nuruodo and smuggler Jorj Car'das||translated=1}} [[File:Chvfighter.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A Vagaari starfighter during the battle of Geroon]] The Vagaari Empire had a large [[Vagaari slaver fleet|slaver fleet]], consisting of at least 200 [[Starship/Legends|vessels]], most of them being [[Warship/Legends|warships]] such as the [[Surfeik cruiser]]s they acquired from the [[Arized homeworld]] the government [[Extermination of the Arized homeworld|exterminated]], and [[Paskla-class cruiser|''Paskla''-class cruisers]]. [[Zero-g plastic bubble]]s covered the [[Hull/Legends|hulls]] of many such ships, storing a slave within them that were used as [[living shield]]s to reduce damage to the Vagaari fleet while in [[Space warfare/Legends|combat]]. [[Vagaari starfighter]]s, that were likewise protected, complemented the slaver fleet. [[Gravity well projector/Legends|Pseudograv projectors]], that they stole from an [[Unidentified species (Pseudograv projector)|unidentified species]], were used to draw others out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|species]] and into what the Vagaari called [[Realspace/Legends|"crawlspace."]] The projectors also prevented the victims from escaping battle. As a nomad species, the Vagaari also used civilian [[Transport/Legends|transports]] and [[Vagaari plunder ship]]s to transport their people, slaves—frozen by [[Vagaari stasis collar]]s that ceased a being's life through [[Suspended animation/Legends|suspended animation]]—and other goods. The slavers' knowledge of other species life was extensive, as they had killed so many, using [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] such as the [[schostri]]—insects whose stings were fatal to most life–forms they were used against—[[Blaster/Legends|blaster pistols]], and [[wolvkil]]s, [[Hair/Legends|furry]] animals they encountered in their travels and domesticated to kill. The Vagaari had no compassion, the wounded were left to die, and supplies were only shared with those who could offer something in return. As they discovered the fact that all species cared for others, the Vagaari exploited this weakness by sending slaves before them when boarding starships, provoking the opponents not to shoot while thinking the Vagaari were willing to negotiate for their lives; however, they were not. The living shields were created after using that tactic during their early travels, and similar strategies were employed during naval warfare—broadcasting those within the zero-g bubbles' cries on public frequencies, allowing the enemy to see the captives and therefore reduce their laserfire, allowing the Vagaari to destroy them in massive counterattacks. To deal damage within an opposing warship, the Vagaari offer crates of time-delayed wolvkils paralyzed by their collars and frozen slaves to lead massive ambushes. ==History== {{Quote|They were a great race of nomadic conquerors and slavers who once flew freely through this region of space, taking and destroying at will, particularly among the smaller species and worlds.|[[Aristocra/Legends|Aristocra]] [[Chaf'orm'bintrano]]|}} A large number of Vagaari forces were destroyed by [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] at the destruction of the ''[[Outbound Flight]]''. After that they retreated to their own corner of the Unknown Regions, recovering from their losses and building up forces for an eventual attack on the Chiss. The Chiss were aware of this buildup, and feared that they had "already made alliances with powers even more dangerous than they are. The Chiss fear was indeed true, as the Vagaari [[Unidentified Miskara (Yuuzhan Vong)|Miskara]] had made contact with a [[Unidentified Yuuzhan Vong representative|representative]] of the "Far Outsiders," the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] species,''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' and helped them plan their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of the galaxy. After the invasion, the Vagaari Empire captured territories once held by those killed by the Yuuzhan Vong. ==Astrography== The Vagaari Empire was nomadic in nature, leaving their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Vagar Praxut]] yet controlled a large area of the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] as a maritime power. ==Appearances== *[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] {{Ab|Outbound Flight (unabridged audiobook)}} *[[Outbound Flight (abridged audiobook)|''Outbound Flight'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' {{Ab|Survivor's Quest (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[Survivor's Quest (abridged audiobook)|''Survivor's Quest'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Vagaari-Imperium |es=Imperio Vagaari }} [[Category:Interplanetary governments]] [[Category:Unknown Regions governments]] [[Category:Vagaari culture]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Vagaari Empire]] }}
